objective: Close F-0029 by executing content AI formal-adoption review persistence, complete question or paper draft creation, all paper composition writes, audit effects, and final adoption target metadata inside one database transaction. Any writer or metadata failure must roll back the full command; committed replay must return the single persisted target without creating duplicates. Preserve the F-0155/F-0156 digest and decision contracts and do not change schema, permissions, Provider behavior, formal content semantics, or public API envelopes.
blockedFiles: question, paper, adoption, audit, or public API business semantics
permission, role, entitlement, Provider, external service, secret/env, deployment, PR or force-push
real database execution or runtime acceptance

authoritativeChainEvidence.requirement: A reviewed content AI result may enter formal content only as one traceable command; partial or duplicate formal drafts violate review lineage and draft integrity.
authoritativeChainEvidence.routeService: The route currently constructs adoption, question, and paper repositories independently; the service then commits adoption, invokes draft writers, and finally updates target metadata as three separate phases.
authoritativeChainEvidence.repository: Question create and paper create/add-question writers are individually transactional and already accept RuntimeDatabaseOptions, but no outer transaction spans the whole adoption command.
authoritativeChainEvidence.database: Drizzle PostgreSQL transaction objects support nested transaction/savepoint calls, so all existing repositories can share one outer transaction through the existing createDatabase injection without schema changes.
authoritativeChainEvidence.rootCause: The production runtime lacks a unit-of-work boundary; service error responses resolve normally, so a wrapper must deliberately roll back on any nonzero response as well as thrown failures.
authoritativeChainEvidence.findingDecision: F-0029 is one atomic command boundary. F-0155 and F-0156 are already closed and remain protected regressions rather than reopened work.

resumeAnchor.task: close the atomic formal-adoption unit of work, then continue the next eligible RC-05 residual
resumeAnchor.condition: do not claim runtime database proof, change schema, or reopen F-0155/F-0156

candidateEvidence.tddRed: 3 expected failures across 8 runtime tests because the transactional command wrapper did not exist
candidateEvidence.focused: 8 files, 77 tests passed; the directly changed service replay test also passed after the fixture type correction
candidateEvidence.typecheck: passed after removing an invalid test-only rejectAction field
candidateEvidence.build: passed with 100 static pages and the pre-existing NFT tracing warning only
candidateEvidence.adversarialReview: passed: production defaults share one outer transaction across adoption, question, paper composition, audits and final metadata; nonzero responses and exceptions roll back; committed replay bypasses all writers; existing paper composition failure and repository transaction regressions remain green; no schema, permission, Provider, dependency or mechanism expansion
